claims.mechanism: The data path is already warm on the local machine, and Bitfield can read by the storage shape that avoids the normal database request path.
notClaiming: A cold disk read takes less than a nanosecond.
Every database benchmark measures the same job.

claims.mechanism: This is the path where Bitfield first resolves the content address before reading the bytes.
notClaiming: This is the same category as the H0 exact read.

claims.mechanism: This row includes more of the saved-write contract than the raw write ceiling, then divides the batch cost across written items.
notClaiming: Every single isolated write has 316ns wall-clock latency.
This is the same category as a networked managed database write.

claims.mechanism: This is the optimized engine path divided across a full engine batch. It is useful as a ceiling, not as a replacement for durable write comparison rows.
notClaiming: A full saved write is always 0.59ns.
This number should be compared directly to every vendor write benchmark.
